title: Injury news: the franchise knee and the shutdown question
arc: somber open, one genuinely heated debate segment in the middle, measured landing

lines.text: This is not the show we planned to make today. The MRI results came back this morning, and they are not good.
Grade two sprain, structural involvement, and a timeline that officially reads week to week, which everyone in that building knows means longer.
Let us be precise, because precision is a kindness here. This is the same knee as two seasons ago, but a different ligament, and that distinction matters.
It matters because the old injury healed clean. This one is new damage, not a recurrence. Small mercy, but a real one.
The comparable cases in the last five years came back in six to nine weeks. The bad outcomes were the guys who came back in four.
And this team, sitting ninth, has every incentive to be tempted by four. That is the conversation nobody wants to have out loud.
So I will say it out loud: shut him down. Full stop. The play-in is not worth a twenty-six-year-old franchise knee, and it is not close.
You cannot make that call from a studio, Max. The medical staff sees the scans, we see a press release with adjectives in it.
The press release says week to week and the team is four games from the deadline. You do not need the scan, you need the incentive chart!
And the player gets a vote! These guys are not cargo. He has said twice this year he wants to play through the stretch run.
Players always want to play, that is why teams employ adults to say no. The organizations that fail here are the ones that outsource the no.

segments.title: What it means for the season

lines.text: Whatever they decide, the next six weeks look different. The offense runs through that spot on the floor, and there is no like-for-like replacement on the roster.
The kid gets his minutes now, ready or not. Sometimes that is how careers start. It is also sometimes how rotations break.
We will track the recovery reporting carefully and skeptically, and we will flag every anonymous-source timeline for exactly what it is.
Get well soon, big fella. The league is better with you in it, and this show is calmer, which frankly we could use.
That is the show. No predictions today. Some weeks the only honest take is patience.
